dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S3582 IS: No Rewards for January 6 Rioters Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Mr. Padilla (for himself, Mr. Whitehouse, Mr. Murphy, Mr. Blumenthal, Mr. Wyden, Mr. Welch, Mr. Kim, Mr. Van Hollen, Ms. Hirono, Mr. Peters, Mr. Durbin, Mr. Markey, Mr. Kelly, Ms. Klobuchar, Mr. Schiff, Mr. Coons, Ms. Cortez Masto, Mrs. Gillibrand, Mrs. Shaheen, Ms. Warren, Mr. Merkley, Mr. Reed, and Mr. Booker) introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on the Judiciary

form.official-title: To prohibit the use of Federal funds to compensate individuals who were prosecuted for their involvement in the attack on the United States Capitol on January 6, 2021, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the No Rewards for January 6 Rioters Act.

section.header: Limitations on uses of Federal funds

subsection.header: Prohibition on use of Federal funds
subsection.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, no Federal funds, including funds appropriated under section 1304 of title 31, United States Code (commonly known as the Judgment Fund), or any victim compensation fund, may be used to compensate any individual prosecuted for involvement in the attack on the United States Capitol on January 6, 2021, including any individual so prosecuted and subsequently pardoned.

subsection.header: Prohibition on the establishment of a compensation fund
subsection.text: No compensation fund shall be established for the purpose of compensating individuals described in subsection (a).

section.header: Restitution and fines not to be refunded

subsection.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, no funds shall be disbursed from the United States Treasury to refund any court-ordered compensation, including restitution, fines, or special assessments, paid by any individual convicted for involvement in the attack on the United States Capitol on January 6, 2021, including any individual so convicted and subsequently pardoned.
The Secretary of the Treasury shall transfer any amounts described in subsection (a) to the Architect of the Capitol.
